alert: Retinol and glycolic acid (AHA) together can cause severe irritation, peeling, and compromise your skin barrier.
fix: Alternate nights — use glycolic acid one evening and retinol the next.

alert: Layering retinol with salicylic acid (BHA) increases the risk of dryness, irritation, and over-exfoliation.
Benzoyl peroxide can oxidize and deactivate retinol, making both ingredients less effective while increasing irritation.
fix: Never combine; use benzoyl peroxide in the morning and retinol in the evening.

alert: Using retinol and vitamin C together can cause irritation for sensitive skin, and they work best at different pH levels.
fix: Use vitamin C in the morning and retinol in the evening for best results.

alert: Combining AHA (glycolic acid) and BHA (salicylic acid) increases the chance of over-exfoliation and irritation.
fix: Use one at a time; don't layer. Try AHA one night and BHA the next, or use them on different areas.

alert: Niacinamide and vitamin C were once thought to cancel each other out, but this is largely a myth. Some people may experience mild flushing.
fix: Wait 15 minutes between application or use at different times of day if you notice any flushing.

alert: Benzoyl peroxide oxidizes vitamin C, rendering it completely ineffective. Both ingredients are wasted when combined.
fix: Never combine; they cancel each other out. Use vitamin C in the morning and benzoyl peroxide in the evening.

alert: Both are acidic actives. Layering them can lower your skin's pH too much, causing stinging, redness, and irritation.
fix: Don't layer; use vitamin C in the morning and glycolic acid in the evening.

alert: Both are acidic and layering them can cause excessive irritation and reduce the effectiveness of vitamin C.
fix: Don't layer; use vitamin C in the morning and salicylic acid in the evening.

alert: Retinol and lactic acid (AHA) together can cause significant irritation, redness, and damage to the skin barrier.
fix: Alternate nights; never layer these two ingredients in the same routine.

alert: Combining benzoyl peroxide with glycolic acid can lead to excessive dryness, peeling, and irritation.
fix: Don't use at the same time; apply them during different parts of your routine (morning vs. evening).

alert: Both are exfoliating acids. Using them together may over-exfoliate and irritate the skin.
fix: Alternate days or use at different times of day to reduce the risk of irritation.
